**[POSTNUPTIAL AGREEMENT]{.underline}**

REVISED DRAFT --- January 6, 2025 \^TC-01\^

**between**

**DIANE VESELKA-THORNTON** (\"Wife\")

**and**

**MARCUS THORNTON** (\"Husband\")

Date of Execution: **\_\_\_\_\_***\_*

PRIVILEGED AND CONFIDENTIAL --- DRAFT

**[ARTICLE I --- RECITALS]{.underline}**

**[Section 1.1 --- Identity of the Parties]{.underline}**

This Postnuptial Agreement (the \"Agreement\") is entered into by and between Diane Veselka-Thornton (\"Wife\"), age forty-four (44), and Marcus Thornton (\"Husband\"), age forty-six (46), collectively referred to herein as the \"Parties\" and individually as a \"Party.\" The Parties currently reside together at the marital residence located at 1847 Birchwood Lane, Winnetka, Illinois 60093. Wife and Husband were lawfully married on June 14, 2013, in a ceremony held in Cook County, Illinois, and their marriage remains in full force and effect as of the date of this Agreement.

Wife is a board-certified pediatric cardiologist employed at Lakeshore Children\'s Hospital, 680 North Lake Shore Drive, Chicago, Illinois 60611, where she has practiced since 2011. Wife is also a member of Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC, an Illinois limited liability company through which she maintains a professional practice interest.

Husband is the Senior Vice President of Business Development at Grayfield Industries, a Delaware corporation with principal offices at 500 Commerce Parkway, Naperville, Illinois 60563, where he has been employed since March 15, 2018. Husband currently holds unvested restricted stock units in Grayfield Industries, as more fully described in Article VII hereof.

Both Parties are citizens and residents of the State of Illinois and have maintained their principal residence in the State of Illinois throughout the course of their marriage. Husband is the surviving child of the late Constance \^TC-02\^ Thornton (née Albrecht), who passed away on September 3, 2024, and from whom Husband received a substantial inheritance, as described in Section 1.6 hereof.

**[Section 1.2 --- Recital of Marriage and Children]{.underline}**

Wife and Husband were lawfully married on June 14, 2013, in Cook County, Illinois. As of the date of this Agreement, the Parties have been married for approximately eleven (11) years. The Parties are the parents of two (2) minor children born of the marriage: Elena Thornton, born on August 22, 2012 (age twelve (12) as of the date hereof), and Julian Thornton, born on November 9, 2015 (age nine (9) as of the date hereof). Both children currently reside with the Parties at the marital residence. The Parties acknowledge that this Agreement is not intended to address \^TC-03\^ and does not address, issues of child custody, child support, or parenting time, all of which shall be determined in accordance with applicable Illinois law in the event of a Dissolution, giving paramount consideration to the best interests of the Children.

Both Parties wish to define their respective property rights and financial obligations during the marriage and in the event of Dissolution, as that term is defined in Article II below, while preserving their commitment to their marital relationship and the well-being of their Children.

**[Section 1.3 --- Recital of Purpose]{.underline}**

The Parties enter this Agreement to clarify property rights and financial obligations following a period of marital difficulty. The Parties enter this Agreement with the shared desire to preserve and strengthen their marital bond, to establish financial clarity, and to promote mutual trust and transparency going forward. Both Parties recognize that open and honest financial communication is essential to the health and longevity of their marriage, and they intend for this Agreement to serve as a foundation for renewed cooperation and partnership in all aspects of their shared financial life. \^TC-04\^

\[MC-01: \"Added language reflecting both parties\' commitment to the marriage. Recitals are non-operative.\" --- TB\]

The Parties have discussed and negotiated the terms of this Agreement over the course of approximately four (4) months, beginning in August 2024, with the assistance of their respective independent legal counsel. This Agreement represents the product of those negotiations and reflects the mutual intent of the Parties to establish a clear and comprehensive framework governing their financial affairs during the marriage and upon any Dissolution thereof. The Parties acknowledge that neither the execution of this Agreement nor any provision contained herein shall be construed as evidence of an intent or desire by either Party to seek or obtain a Dissolution of the marriage.

**[Section 1.4 --- Recital of Independent Legal Counsel]{.underline}**

Each Party has been represented by independent legal counsel of their own choosing throughout the negotiation, drafting, and execution of this Agreement. Wife has been represented by Catherine Odom, Partner, Kessler, Odom & Pratt LLP, 210 South Wacker Drive, Suite 2400, Chicago, Illinois 60606. Husband has been represented by Thomas Baxter, Partner, Hargrove Baxter LLP, 150 North Michigan Avenue, Suite 1800 \^TC-05\^, Chicago, Illinois 60601. Each Party represents that they have had a full and fair opportunity to consult with their respective counsel regarding all aspects of this Agreement and that they have received advice from their respective counsel regarding their legal rights and obligations hereunder. Each Party has been afforded sufficient time and opportunity to review, consider, and discuss the terms and provisions of this Agreement with counsel prior to execution.

**[Section 1.5 --- Recital of Financial Disclosure]{.underline}**

Both Parties acknowledge having received substantially \^TC-06\^ full financial disclosure from the other Party. Each Party has provided the other with a comprehensive financial disclosure, including detailed information regarding assets, liabilities, income, and expenses, prepared with the assistance of Linden & Fosse CPA, 440 West Randolph Street, Suite 600, Chicago, Illinois 60606. Wife\'s financial disclosure is attached hereto as Schedule A and incorporated by reference. Husband\'s financial disclosure is attached hereto as Schedule B and incorporated by reference. Each Party represents that they have had a reasonable and adequate opportunity to review the financial information provided by the other Party and to ask questions concerning the other Party\'s financial condition. Both Parties have been assisted in their review by their respective independent legal counsel and, where appropriate, by their independent financial advisors.

**[Section 1.6 --- Recital of Husband\'s Inheritance]{.underline}**

In or about October 2024, following the death of Constance Thornton (née Albrecht) on September 3, 2024, Husband received an inheritance (the \"Inheritance\") consisting of the following assets:

> \(a\) A lakefront vacation property located at 2291 Lakeshore Road, Ephraim, Wisconsin 54211 (the \"Door County Property\"), appraised at a fair market value of One Million Four Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars (\$1,450,000) by Midwest Appraisal Associates as of October 15, 2024;
>
> \(b\) A brokerage account held at Tidewater Securities (account ending in -7724), with a value of One Million Nine Hundred Twenty Thousand Dollars (\$1,920,000) as of October 31, 2024; and
>
> \(c\) Personal property and jewelry, valued in the aggregate at Four Hundred Thirty Thousand Dollars (\$430,000) per the estate inventory filed with the Door County Probate Court.

The total aggregate value of the Inheritance is approximately Three Million Eight Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$3,800,000). The Inheritance was distributed to Husband as the sole beneficiary under the Last Will and Testament of Constance Thornton, and the disposition thereof is addressed in Article IV of this Agreement.

**[ARTICLE II --- DEFINITIONS]{.underline}**

**[Section 2.1 --- Defined Terms]{.underline}**

As used in this Agreement, the following terms shall have the meanings set forth below:

**(a) \"Agreement\"** \^TC-07\^ shall mean this Postnuptial Agreement, together with all schedules and exhibits attached hereto and incorporated herein by reference, as the same may be amended from time to time in accordance with Article XIV hereof.

**(b) \"Marital Property\"** shall mean all property acquired by either party during the marriage from Earned Income or joint efforts, regardless of title. \^TC-08\^ For the avoidance of doubt, Marital Property shall not include Separate Property as defined in subsection (c) below, except to the extent that Separate Property has been reclassified as Marital Property in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.

**(c) \"Separate Property\"** \^TC-09\^ shall mean (i) property owned by a Party prior to the marriage; (ii) property acquired by a Party during the marriage by gift, legacy, or descent; (iii) property acquired by a Party in exchange for or traceable to property described in clauses (i) or (ii); (iv) property designated as the Separate Property of a Party by the terms of this Agreement; and (v) any increase in the value of Separate Property that remains classified as Separate Property under this Agreement and applicable law.

**(d) \"Earned Income\"** shall mean the base salary, regular wages, and standard employment compensation received by a Party in exchange for services rendered to such Party\'s employer or, in the case of self-employment, the regular draws or guaranteed payments received from a professional practice or business entity. Earned Income shall not include discretionary bonuses, commissions, equity compensation, performance incentives, restricted stock or restricted stock units, stock options, or other forms of variable or contingent compensation. \^TC-10\^

\[MC-02: \"New definition added for clarity in calculating contribution obligations. Standard usage.\" --- TB\]

**(e) \"Dissolution\"** \^TC-11\^ shall mean the entry of a final judgment of dissolution of marriage, declaration of invalidity, or legal separation by a court of competent jurisdiction with respect to the marriage of the Parties, including any judgment entered in connection with proceedings under the Illinois Marriage and Dissolution of Marriage Act, 750 ILCS 5/101 et seq.

**(f) \"Effective Date\"** \^TC-12\^ shall mean the date on which this Agreement has been executed by both Parties in the presence of witnesses and, if applicable, acknowledged before a notary public, as set forth in Article XVI hereof.

**(g) \"Net Worth\"** shall mean the total fair market value of all assets owned by a Party, whether Marital Property or Separate Property, reduced by all outstanding liabilities owed by such Party as of the applicable date of determination. \^TC-13\^

**(h) \"Children\"** \^TC-14\^ shall mean Elena Thornton (born August 22, 2012) and Julian Thornton (born November 9, 2015), the minor children of the Parties born of the marriage.

**(i) \"Identified Debt\"** shall mean the credit card debt totaling approximately Sixty-Eight Thousand Dollars (\$68,000) maintained by Husband, as more fully described in Section 11.2.

**(j) \"Schedules\"** shall mean Schedules A and B attached hereto and incorporated herein by reference, which set forth the financial disclosure summaries of Wife and Husband, respectively.

**(k) \"Party\" or \"Parties\"** shall mean Wife and Husband individually and collectively, as the context requires.

**[ARTICLE III --- REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES]{.underline}**

**[Section 3.1 --- Mutual Representations]{.underline}**

Each Party represents and warrants to the other as follows:

> \(a\) Such Party is entering this Agreement voluntarily, without duress, coercion, or undue influence of any kind, and has freely and willingly consented to all of the terms and provisions hereof;
>
> \(b\) Such Party has had adequate time, not less than sixty (60) days, to review and consider the terms of this Agreement prior to execution;
>
> \(c\) Such Party has been represented by independent legal counsel of their own choosing throughout the negotiation, drafting, and execution of this Agreement;
>
> \(d\) Each Party has had a reasonable opportunity to review the financial information provided by the other Party and is generally satisfied with such disclosure; \^TC-15\^
>
> \(e\) Such Party understands the legal consequences and effects of this Agreement, including the rights and obligations being assumed, modified, or waived hereunder; and
>
> \(f\) Such Party has had a full and fair opportunity to ask questions and receive answers regarding the other Party\'s financial condition, assets, liabilities, income, and expenses.

**[Section 3.2 --- No Duress or Undue Influence]{.underline}**

Each Party specifically represents and warrants that no duress, fraud, coercion, or undue influence has been exerted upon or against such Party in connection with the negotiation, drafting, or execution of this Agreement. Each Party acknowledges that the terms hereof have been arrived at through arms-length negotiation between the Parties and their respective independent legal counsel and that this Agreement is fair, just, and reasonable under the circumstances existing as of the Effective Date.

**[ARTICLE IV --- SEPARATE PROPERTY]{.underline}**

**[Section 4.1 --- Wife\'s Separate Property]{.underline}**

Wife\'s premarital savings, originally totaling approximately One Million Two Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$1,200,000), which funds have been commingled into the joint investment account and are now treated as Marital Property pursuant to Section 5.1, \^TC-16\^ were accumulated by Wife prior to the date of marriage from earnings, savings, and investment returns. The Parties acknowledge that these premarital savings were contributed by Wife to the joint investment account managed by Northbridge Wealth Advisors (account ending in -3961), which account currently has a total value of approximately One Million Eight Hundred Seventy Thousand Dollars (\$1,870,000).

Wife holds a twenty-two percent (22%) membership interest in Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC, an Illinois limited liability company organized under the Illinois Limited Liability Company Act, 805 ILCS 180/1-1 et seq. Wife\'s membership interest has been valued at One Million Six Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars (\$1,650,000) as of November 30, 2024, per the independent valuation report prepared by Strand Valuation Services, a copy of which has been provided to Husband and his counsel. Wife\'s membership interest in Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC shall be classified as Wife\'s Separate Property to the extent of its value as of the Effective Date, subject to the provisions of Article VI governing appreciation in value.

All other property owned by Wife prior to the marriage, to the extent not commingled with Marital Property and still identifiable and traceable, shall remain Wife\'s Separate Property.

**[Section 4.2 --- Husband\'s Separate Property]{.underline}**

Husband\'s inheritance from the late Constance Thornton (née Albrecht), totaling approximately Three Million Eight Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$3,800,000) as described in Section 1.6, shall be classified as Husband\'s Separate Property. The Inheritance is itemized as follows:

> \(a\) \^TC-17\^ The Door County Property, being the lakefront vacation home located at 2291 Lakeshore Road, Ephraim, Wisconsin 54211, appraised at a fair market value of One Million Four Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars (\$1,450,000);
>
> \(b\) The brokerage account held at Tidewater Securities (account ending in -7724), valued at One Million Nine Hundred Twenty Thousand Dollars (\$1,920,000) as of October 31, 2024; and
>
> \(c\) Personal property and jewelry, valued in the aggregate at Four Hundred Thirty Thousand Dollars (\$430,000).

Husband\'s Separate Property as described in this Section 4.2 shall remain his sole and exclusive property throughout the term of this Agreement and shall not be subject to division, distribution, or equitable allocation upon Dissolution, except as otherwise specifically provided in this Agreement. Husband shall have the sole and exclusive right to manage, control, sell, transfer, encumber, or otherwise dispose of his Separate Property without the consent or joinder of Wife.

**[Section 4.3 --- Maintenance of Separate Property Character]{.underline}**

\(a\) Each Party shall use reasonable efforts to maintain their respective Separate Property in accounts, titles, and registrations held solely in that Party\'s individual name or in the name of a trust, entity, or nominee under that Party\'s sole control. The Parties acknowledge that the maintenance of clear title and ownership records is essential to the preservation of the separate character of their respective Separate Property.

\(b\) Subject to subsection (c) below, commingling of Separate Property with Marital Property may, depending on the circumstances, result in the reclassification of such property, provided that tracing of the separate property component remains possible under applicable law. \^TC-18\^

\[MC-03: \"Original language was overbroad. Revised to align with Illinois tracing principles under 750 ILCS 5/503(c).\" --- TB\]

\(c\) Notwithstanding any provision of this Agreement to the contrary, separate property used for the joint benefit of the marital household, including but not limited to mortgage payments, home improvements, family travel and vacations, educational expenses for the Children, or maintenance of jointly-used property, shall not be deemed commingled solely by reason of such use, and the Party contributing such separate property shall retain a claim for reimbursement of such amounts from the marital estate, with such property retaining its classification as Separate Property of the contributing Party. \^TC-19\^

\[MC-04: \"New provision to protect both parties\' separate property from inadvertent commingling through ordinary family expenditures.\" --- TB\]

**[ARTICLE V --- MARITAL PROPERTY]{.underline}**

**[Section 5.1 --- Classification of Marital Property]{.underline}**

All property acquired by either Party during the marriage from Earned Income or joint efforts, regardless of title \^TC-08\^ (as referenced from the definition in Section 2.1(b)), other than Separate Property as defined in Article IV, shall be classified as Marital Property. Marital Property includes, but is not limited to, the following: \^TC-21\^

> \(a\) The marital residence located at 1847 Birchwood Lane, Winnetka, Illinois 60093, with a net equity of approximately One Million Two Hundred Sixty-Three Thousand Dollars (\$1,263,000), based upon a fair market value of One Million Eight Hundred Seventy-Five Thousand Dollars (\$1,875,000), as determined by the appraisal report of Pendleton Appraisal Group dated November 15, 2024, less the outstanding mortgage balance of approximately Six Hundred Twelve Thousand Dollars (\$612,000) held by Prairie State Title Company;
>
> \(b\) Joint savings and checking accounts held at First Saxonbrook Bank and Hollcroft Fiduciary, with a combined balance of approximately One Hundred Eighty-Seven Thousand Dollars (\$187,000);
>
> \(c\) Contributions to retirement accounts made by either Party during the marriage, including but not limited to employer matching contributions and the appreciation thereof, to the extent classified as Marital Property under Article VIII;
>
> \(d\) The joint investment account managed by Northbridge Wealth Advisors (current value One Million Eight Hundred Seventy Thousand Dollars (\$1,870,000)) \^TC-20\^ (account ending in -3961); and
>
> \(e\) All other property, real or personal, tangible or intangible, acquired by either Party during the marriage and not otherwise classified as Separate Property under this Agreement.

**[\[DELETED: Section 5.2 --- Wife\'s Premarital Savings Reimbursement Right\] \^TC-22\^]{.underline}**

\[MC-05: \"Section deleted --- duplicative of Section 5.1 general property division provisions.\" --- TB\]

\^TC-23\^ *\[Subsequent sections renumbered.\]*

**[Section 5.2 --- Division of Marital Property Upon Dissolution \^TC-24\^]{.underline}**

Upon Dissolution, Marital Property shall be divided equitably between the Parties, with a presumption of equal division (fifty percent (50%) to each Party), unless the Parties agree otherwise in writing or a court of competent jurisdiction determines that an alternative allocation is appropriate under the factors set forth in Section 503 of the Illinois Marriage and Dissolution of Marriage Act, 750 ILCS 5/503. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to limit the authority of a court to consider all relevant factors in determining an equitable division of Marital Property, except to the extent that such authority is expressly limited by the specific provisions of this Agreement governing the classification and treatment of identified assets.

Each Party shall cooperate fully and in good faith in effectuating the division of Marital Property upon Dissolution, including by executing all documents, instruments, and conveyances reasonably necessary to transfer title and ownership of assets in accordance with the agreed-upon or court-ordered division.

**[ARTICLE VI --- WIFE\'S PROFESSIONAL PRACTICE INTEREST]{.underline}**

**[Section 6.1 --- Classification of Practice Interest]{.underline}**

Wife\'s twenty-two percent (22%) membership interest in Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC, an Illinois limited liability company (the \"Practice Interest\"), is classified as Wife\'s Separate Property to the extent of its value as of the Effective Date, being One Million Six Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars (\$1,650,000) per the independent valuation report prepared by Strand Valuation Services dated November 30, 2024 (the \"Baseline Value\").

Any appreciation in the value of Wife\'s interest in Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC above the baseline value of One Million Six Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars (\$1,650,000) during the term of this Agreement shall be classified as Marital Property subject to division under Section 5.2, but only to the extent such appreciation is directly attributable to the active personal efforts of Wife during the marriage, as determined by a qualified forensic valuation expert mutually agreed upon by the Parties (or, absent agreement, appointed by the court). Appreciation attributable to market forces, changes in insurance reimbursement rates, regulatory changes, passive investment returns, or other factors not directly resulting from Wife\'s active personal efforts shall remain the Separate Property of Wife. \^TC-25\^

\[MC-06: \"Revised to reflect standard active/passive appreciation distinction recognized in Illinois case law. Protects Wife\'s separate property interest from overreaching marital claims.\" --- TB\]

The Parties acknowledge that the Practice Interest is subject to the terms and conditions of the Operating Agreement of Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC, as amended from time to time, including without limitation any restrictions on transfer, buy-sell provisions, and rights of first refusal contained therein. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to require Wife to violate the terms of the Operating Agreement or to compel the transfer of the Practice Interest to Husband or any third party.

**[Section 6.2 --- Valuation Methodology]{.underline}**

If a valuation of the Practice Interest is required in connection with a Dissolution or other event requiring a determination of value under this Agreement, such valuation shall be performed using the same methodology employed by Strand Valuation Services in its November 30, 2024 report or such other qualified valuation firm as the Parties may agree \^TC-26\^, including but not limited to the income approach, the market approach, and any applicable discounts for lack of marketability and minority interest. The costs of any such valuation shall be shared equally by the Parties unless otherwise agreed or ordered by the court. Each Party shall cooperate fully with the retained valuation expert by providing access to all financial records, tax returns, and other documents reasonably necessary to complete the valuation.

**[ARTICLE VII --- HUSBAND\'S EQUITY COMPENSATION (RSUs)]{.underline}**

**[Section 7.1 --- Classification and Division of RSUs]{.underline}**

Husband\'s 12,000 unvested restricted stock units (\"RSUs\") in Grayfield Industries, a Delaware corporation, granted on January 1, 2024, and vesting in equal annual installments of 4,000 shares on each of January 1, 2025, January 1, 2026, and January 1, 2027, shall be classified as Marital Property to the extent earned during the marriage. Wife\'s share of each vesting tranche shall be determined by applying a coverture fraction, calculated as follows: the numerator shall be the number of months of marriage from the commencement of the Relevant Service Period through the date of Dissolution (or date of vesting, whichever is earlier), and the denominator shall be the total number of months in the Relevant Service Period. Wife shall receive fifty percent (50%) of the resulting coverture fraction of each tranche. For purposes of this Section, the \"Relevant Service Period\" shall be defined as the period beginning on Husband\'s date of hire with Grayfield Industries (March 15, 2018) through the final vesting date of the applicable RSU tranche. \^TC-27\^

\[MC-07: \"Revised to apply standard coverture fraction methodology used in Illinois for division of equity compensation. More equitable than a blanket 50/50 split.\" --- TB\]

The Parties acknowledge that the current fair market value of Grayfield Industries common stock is approximately Thirty-Eight Dollars (\$38.00) per share, resulting in a total current estimated value of the unvested RSUs of approximately Four Hundred Fifty-Six Thousand Dollars (\$456,000). The Parties further acknowledge that the actual value of the RSUs upon vesting will depend on the market price of Grayfield Industries common stock at the time of vesting and may be higher or lower than the current estimated value. Husband shall provide Wife with written notice of each vesting event within ten (10) business days of such event, together with documentation reflecting the number of shares vested, the applicable tax withholding, and the net shares or cash proceeds received.

**[Section 7.2 --- Tax Treatment]{.underline}**

Each Party shall bear the tax consequences attributable to their respective share of the RSU distributions. \^TC-28\^ Husband shall be responsible for any tax withholding required at the time of vesting, and Wife\'s share shall be calculated based on the net after-tax value of each vesting tranche, unless the Parties agree to an alternative tax allocation. Husband shall provide Wife with all tax documentation reasonably necessary for Wife to report her share of the RSU distributions on her federal and state income tax returns within thirty (30) days of each vesting event.

**[ARTICLE VIII --- RETIREMENT ACCOUNTS]{.underline}**

**[Section 8.1 --- Wife\'s Retirement Accounts]{.underline}**

\^TC-29\^ Wife maintains the following retirement accounts: (i) a 403(b) retirement savings plan through Lakeshore Children\'s Hospital, administered by TIAA-CREF, and (ii) a defined-benefit pension plan through Lakeshore Children\'s Hospital. The combined current value of Wife\'s retirement accounts is approximately Two Million Three Hundred Forty Thousand Dollars (\$2,340,000), as set forth in Schedule A. The Parties agree that the balance of Wife\'s retirement accounts as of the Effective Date shall be classified as Wife\'s Separate Property. All contributions made to Wife\'s retirement accounts after the Effective Date during the marriage, together with any appreciation, earnings, or growth attributable to such post-Effective Date contributions, shall be classified as Marital Property and shall be subject to equal division upon Dissolution in accordance with Section 5.2 of this Agreement. For purposes of this Section, the present value of the defined-benefit pension shall be determined by an actuary mutually agreed upon by the Parties or, absent agreement, appointed by the court.

**[Section 8.2 --- Husband\'s Retirement Account]{.underline}**

\^TC-30\^ Husband maintains a 401(k) retirement savings plan through Grayfield Industries, administered by Hartleigh Investments, with a current value of approximately One Million One Hundred Eighty Thousand Dollars (\$1,180,000), as set forth in Schedule B. The Parties agree that the balance of Husband\'s 401(k) account as of the Effective Date shall be classified as Husband\'s Separate Property. All contributions made to Husband\'s 401(k) account after the Effective Date during the marriage, including employer matching contributions, together with any appreciation, earnings, or growth attributable to such post-Effective Date contributions, shall be classified as Marital Property and shall be subject to equal division upon Dissolution in accordance with Section 5.2 of this Agreement.

**[Section 8.3 --- Qualified Domestic Relations Orders]{.underline}**

Division of retirement accounts, if required upon Dissolution, shall be accomplished \^TC-31\^ through Qualified Domestic Relations Orders (\"QDROs\") or such other applicable orders or instruments as may be required by the plan administrators or by applicable federal or state law. Each Party shall cooperate fully and in good faith in the preparation and submission of any necessary QDROs or other division orders, and each Party shall bear one-half (1/2) of the costs associated with the preparation and filing of such orders.

**[ARTICLE IX --- MARITAL RESIDENCE]{.underline}**

**[Section 9.1 --- Description and Current Status]{.underline}**

\^TC-32\^ The marital residence is located at 1847 Birchwood Lane, Winnetka, Illinois 60093, Cook County, Illinois (the \"Marital Residence\"). The Marital Residence was purchased by the Parties in 2014 for a purchase price of One Million Three Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars (\$1,350,000). The current fair market value of the Marital Residence is One Million Eight Hundred Seventy-Five Thousand Dollars (\$1,875,000), as determined by the independent appraisal report prepared by Pendleton Appraisal Group dated November 15, 2024. The Marital Residence is subject to an outstanding mortgage in the approximate amount of Six Hundred Twelve Thousand Dollars (\$612,000), held by Prairie State Title Company. The resulting net equity in the Marital Residence is approximately One Million Two Hundred Sixty-Three Thousand Dollars (\$1,263,000). Title to the Marital Residence is held jointly by the Parties as tenants by the entirety.

**[Section 9.2 --- Disposition Upon Dissolution]{.underline}**

Upon Dissolution, Wife shall have a first right of purchase of the Marital Residence at fair market value (as determined by an independent appraisal obtained within sixty (60) days of the commencement of dissolution proceedings, at the joint expense of the Parties). If Wife elects to exercise her right of purchase, she shall notify Husband in writing within thirty (30) days of receipt of the appraisal, and she shall buy out Husband\'s equity share (being fifty percent (50%) of the net equity after satisfaction of the outstanding mortgage and customary costs of transfer) payable over eighteen (18) months \^TC-34\^ from the date of Dissolution, with such buyout payment secured by a lien on the property, \^TC-33\^ with interest accruing on the unpaid balance at the applicable federal rate in effect on the date of Dissolution, compounded monthly. If Wife fails to exercise her right of purchase within the thirty (30) day period, Husband shall have the same right of purchase on the same terms. If neither Party elects to purchase the Marital Residence, the property shall be listed for sale with a mutually agreed-upon licensed real estate broker within sixty (60) days, at a listing price consistent with the appraised fair market value, and the net proceeds of sale (after satisfaction of the mortgage, customary closing costs, and broker commissions) shall be divided equally between the Parties on a fifty-fifty (50/50) basis.

During the period of any buyout, the purchasing Party shall be solely responsible for all mortgage payments, property taxes, insurance, and ordinary maintenance of the Marital Residence.

**[ARTICLE X --- SPOUSAL MAINTENANCE]{.underline}**

**[Section 10.1 --- Eligibility for Maintenance]{.underline}**

\^TC-35\^ In the event of Dissolution, the lower-earning spouse may seek spousal maintenance in accordance with the provisions of the Illinois Marriage and Dissolution of Marriage Act, 750 ILCS 5/504, as modified by the specific terms and limitations set forth in this Article X. The Parties intend for this Article to supplement, rather than wholly supplant, the statutory framework governing maintenance, except to the extent that specific provisions hereof expressly modify or limit statutory entitlements.

**[Section 10.2 --- Duration Cap]{.underline}**

Maintenance, if awarded, shall not exceed a period of three (3) years \^TC-36\^ from the date of entry of the judgment of dissolution. This limitation on duration shall apply regardless of the length of the marriage at the time of Dissolution and shall supersede any longer period that might otherwise be available under 750 ILCS 5/504(b-1).

\[MC-08: \"Adjusted duration to reflect more common postnuptial maintenance terms. Five years excessive given both parties\' earning capacity.\" --- TB\]

**[Section 10.3 --- \[DELETED: Reserved\] \[ADDED: Automatic Waiver Provision\] \^TC-37\^]{.underline}**

Notwithstanding any other provision of this Article, the right to seek or receive spousal maintenance shall be automatically and irrevocably waived if the spouse who would otherwise be eligible to receive maintenance has a gross annual income (from all sources, including but not limited to Earned Income, investment income, and distributions) exceeding Two Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$200,000) as of the date of entry of the judgment of dissolution, as documented by such spouse\'s most recent federal income tax return or, if more recent, current pay records. For purposes of this Section, \"gross annual income\" shall include salary, wages, bonuses, commissions, investment income, rental income, business income, distributions from partnerships or limited liability companies, and all other forms of income reportable on such spouse\'s federal income tax return. The Parties acknowledge and agree that this provision is intended to ensure that spousal maintenance is reserved for the Party who genuinely requires financial support following Dissolution and that the waiver imposed by this Section is fair and reasonable under the circumstances.

\[MC-09: \"New provision ensures maintenance is reserved for the spouse who genuinely needs financial support. Benefits both parties equally.\" --- TB\]

**[Section 10.4 --- Maintenance Calculation]{.underline}**

If spousal maintenance is payable under this Article X, the amount of such maintenance shall be calculated in accordance with the statutory formula set forth in 750 ILCS 5/504(b-1), provided that for purposes of such calculation, the income of each Party shall be determined by reference to such Party\'s Earned Income as defined in Section 2.1(d) \^TC-38\^ for the calendar year immediately preceding the date of Dissolution (or, if the Party\'s income for such year is not yet determinable, the most recent calendar year for which complete income data is available). In no event shall the maintenance amount calculated under this Section, when combined with the income of the recipient spouse, exceed forty percent (40%) of the combined income of the Parties, consistent with the statutory cap under Illinois law.

**[ARTICLE XI --- ALLOCATION OF DEBTS]{.underline}**

**[Section 11.1 --- General Principle]{.underline}**

\^TC-39\^ Each Party shall be solely responsible for all debts, obligations, and liabilities incurred solely in such Party\'s individual name, whether incurred before or during the marriage. Joint debts incurred by the Parties during the marriage shall be divided equitably upon Dissolution, with a presumption of equal responsibility unless the Parties agree otherwise or a court of competent jurisdiction determines that an alternative allocation is appropriate. Each Party shall indemnify and hold the other Party harmless from any liability arising from such Party\'s individual debts.

**[Section 11.2 --- Husband\'s Undisclosed Credit Card Debt (Identified Debt)]{.underline}**

The Parties acknowledge that during the course of the marriage, Husband maintained credit card accounts and incurred credit card debt totaling approximately Sixty-Eight Thousand Dollars (\$68,000) (the \"Identified Debt\") without the knowledge or consent of Wife. The Identified Debt consists of balances on multiple revolving credit accounts held in Husband\'s individual name with various credit card issuers. Husband assumes sole and complete responsibility for the Identified Debt, including all principal, interest, fees, penalties, and charges accrued or accruing thereon. Husband shall indemnify and hold Wife harmless from claims arising from the Identified Debt, provided that such claims are material and exceed Five Thousand Dollars (\$5,000) individually. Husband\'s indemnification obligation under this Section shall not extend to claims, demands, or collection actions that individually assert amounts less than Five Thousand Dollars (\$5,000). \^TC-40\^

\[MC-10: \"Added de minimis threshold to prevent frivolous indemnification claims. Standard commercial practice.\" --- TB\]

Husband shall use his best efforts to satisfy the Identified Debt in full within twenty-four (24) months of the Effective Date of this Agreement and shall provide Wife with quarterly written statements reflecting the outstanding balance of the Identified Debt until such time as it is fully satisfied. If Husband fails to satisfy the Identified Debt within the twenty-four (24) month period, Wife may, but shall not be obligated to, satisfy any outstanding balance and seek reimbursement from Husband, subject to the limitations set forth in this Section.

**[ARTICLE XII --- FINANCIAL DISCLOSURE]{.underline}**

**[Section 12.1 --- Duty of Disclosure]{.underline}**

Each Party has provided the other with a substantially complete \^TC-41\^ statement of assets, liabilities, income, and expenses, as set forth in the financial disclosure summaries prepared by Linden & Fosse CPA, 440 West Randolph Street, Suite 600, Chicago, Illinois 60606, and attached hereto as Schedules A and B. Each Party represents that the information contained in their respective Schedule is true and correct in all material respects as of the date set forth therein.

**[Section 12.2 --- Consequences of Non-Disclosure]{.underline}**

This Agreement shall be voidable only if a Party can demonstrate, by clear and convincing evidence, that the other Party intentionally concealed assets, income, or liabilities with an aggregate value exceeding One Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$100,000) in the financial disclosure provided in connection with this Agreement. Inadvertent errors or omissions shall not constitute grounds for avoidance of this Agreement. \^TC-42\^

The right to seek avoidance of this Agreement on grounds of non-disclosure must be exercised, if at all, within two (2) years of the date on which the non-offending Party discovered, or reasonably should have discovered, the alleged non-disclosure. Failure to exercise such right within the two-year period shall constitute a waiver of any claim for avoidance on such grounds.

**[ARTICLE XIII --- ENFORCEMENT AND ATTORNEY\'S FEES]{.underline}**

**[Section 13.1 --- Enforcement]{.underline}**

\^TC-43\^ Either Party may seek enforcement of this Agreement in a court of competent jurisdiction. The Parties agree that this Agreement shall be enforceable in accordance with its terms and that specific performance shall be available as a remedy for any breach hereof, in addition to any other remedies available at law or in equity.

**[Section 13.2 --- Attorney\'s Fees]{.underline}**

In any action to enforce or interpret this Agreement, each Party shall bear their own attorney\'s fees and costs, regardless of outcome. Neither Party shall seek an award of attorney\'s fees from the other in connection with any proceeding arising under or related to this Agreement. \^TC-44\^

\[MC-11: \"Mutual waiver of fees promotes amicable resolution and discourages unnecessary litigation. Balanced provision.\" --- TB\]

**[ARTICLE XIV --- MODIFICATION AND AMENDMENT]{.underline}**

**[Section 14.1 --- Requirement for Written Modification]{.underline}**

This Agreement may not be modified, amended, or supplemented except as provided in this Article XIV. \^TC-45\^

**[Section 14.2 --- \[ADDED: Modification by Written Notice\] \^TC-46\^]{.underline}**

Notwithstanding Section 14.1, either Party may propose a modification to this Agreement by delivering written notice to the other Party specifying the proposed modification in reasonable detail. Such proposed modification shall become effective ninety (90) days after delivery of such notice, unless the other Party delivers a written objection to the proposing Party within such ninety (90) day period. If a timely written objection is delivered, the proposed modification shall not take effect and the Parties shall negotiate the proposed modification in good faith. For purposes of this Section, \"delivery\" shall mean personal delivery, delivery by nationally recognized overnight courier, or delivery by certified mail, return receipt requested, to the other Party\'s last known residential address.

**[ARTICLE XV --- GENERAL PROVISIONS]{.underline}**

**[Section 15.1 --- Governing Law]{.underline}**

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Illinois, including without limitation the Illinois Marriage and Dissolution of Marriage Act, 750 ILCS 5/502, 750 ILCS 5/503, and 750 ILCS 5/504, and all other applicable provisions of Illinois law. In the event of any conflict between the provisions of this Agreement and the mandatory provisions of applicable Illinois law, the mandatory provisions of applicable Illinois law shall control.

**[Section 15.2 --- Venue and Forum Selection]{.underline}**

Any action arising under or in connection with this Agreement shall be brought exclusively in the Circuit Court of DuPage County \^TC-47\^, Illinois. Each Party hereby consents to the exclusive jurisdiction and venue of such court and waives any objection based on inconvenient forum or lack of jurisdiction.

\[MC-12: \"DuPage preferred for scheduling efficiency. Husband\'s place of employment is in DuPage County.\" --- TB\]

**[Section 15.3 --- Severability]{.underline}**

If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable by a court of competent jurisdiction, such invalidity, illegality, or unenforceability shall not affect any other provision of this Agreement, and the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect. In the event that any provision is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable, the Parties shall negotiate in good faith to replace such provision with a valid, legal, and enforceable provision that, to the greatest extent possible, achieves the original intent of the Parties as reflected in the invalidated provision.

**[Section 15.4 --- Entire Agreement]{.underline}**

This Agreement, together with all schedules and exhibits attached hereto and incorporated herein by reference, constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior negotiations, discussions, representations, understandings, and agreements, whether oral or written, between the Parties relating to the subject matter hereof. No representations, promises, or inducements have been made by either Party that are not set forth in this Agreement.

**[Section 15.5 --- Sunset Clause]{.underline}**

This Agreement shall terminate automatically on the seventh (7th) anniversary of the Effective Date (the \"Sunset Date\"). If the Agreement is executed on or about June 14, 2025, the Sunset Date shall be approximately June 2032. Upon the Sunset Date, this Agreement shall be of no further force or effect, and all rights and obligations of the Parties hereunder shall terminate, except for any rights or obligations that accrued prior to the Sunset Date and remain unsatisfied. The termination of this Agreement on the Sunset Date shall not affect the validity of any division, transfer, or allocation of property made pursuant to the terms of this Agreement prior to the Sunset Date.

**[ARTICLE XVI --- EXECUTION]{.underline}**

**IN WITNESS WHEREOF**, the Parties have executed this Postnuptial Agreement as of the date set forth below, intending to be legally bound hereby.

**[SUMMARY VALUATION REPORT]{.underline}**

**[22% Membership Interest in Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC]{.underline}**

**An Illinois Limited Liability Company**

Prepared by:

**STRAND VALUATION SERVICES** 135 South LaSalle Street, Suite 3100 Chicago, Illinois 60603

Prepared for:

**Catherine Odom, Partner** Kessler, Odom & Pratt LLP 200 South Wacker Drive, Suite 2400 Chicago, Illinois 60606

On Behalf of:

**Diane Veselka-Thornton**

**Valuation Date: November 30, 2024**

**Report Date: November 30, 2024**

*Engagement Type: Summary valuation report for use in connection with postnuptial agreement negotiations*

**CONFIDENTIALITY NOTICE:** This report is intended solely for the use of the client, Diane Veselka-Thornton, and her legal counsel, Kessler, Odom & Pratt LLP, in connection with the above-referenced matter. This report should not be distributed, reproduced, or used for any other purpose without the prior written consent of Strand Valuation Services. The information contained herein is privileged and confidential.

**[SECTION 1: LETTER OF TRANSMITTAL]{.underline}**

November 30, 2024

Catherine Odom, Partner Kessler, Odom & Pratt LLP 200 South Wacker Drive, Suite 2400 Chicago, Illinois 60606

Dear Ms. Odom:

At your request and pursuant to our engagement agreement dated September 16, 2024, Strand Valuation Services has performed an independent valuation of a 22% membership interest in Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC, an Illinois limited liability company engaged in the practice of pediatric cardiology and general pediatric medicine in suburban Chicago, Illinois. This engagement was undertaken on behalf of your client, Diane Veselka-Thornton, for purposes of the negotiation and execution of a postnuptial agreement.

Based upon our analyses, and subject to the assumptions, limiting conditions, and qualifications set forth in this report, it is our opinion that the fair market value of the 22% membership interest held by Dr. Veselka-Thornton in Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC, as of November 30, 2024, is:

**\$1,650,000** **(One Million Six Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars)**

This report is a summary valuation report prepared in accordance with the professional standards of the American Society of Appraisers and the Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice (USPAP). A summary report does not include all of the analyses, data, and supporting documentation that would be present in a comprehensive (detailed) report. Supporting workpapers and documentation are maintained in our files and are available upon request.

We appreciate the opportunity to be of service in this matter.

Respectfully submitted,

**STRAND VALUATION SERVICES**

By: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

Robert M. Strand, ASA, CFA Senior Managing Director Strand Valuation Services

**[SECTION 2: PURPOSE AND SCOPE OF ENGAGEMENT]{.underline}**

Strand Valuation Services was retained by the law firm of Kessler, Odom & Pratt LLP, on behalf of its client Diane Veselka-Thornton, to provide an independent opinion of the fair market value of Dr. Veselka-Thornton\'s 22% membership interest in Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC (hereinafter, the \"Practice\" or \"Heartbeat Pediatric Partners\"). The purpose of this valuation is for use in the negotiation and execution of a postnuptial agreement between Diane Veselka-Thornton (age 44), a board-certified pediatric cardiologist, and her husband, Marcus Thornton (age 46), Senior Vice President of Business Development at Grayfield Industries, a publicly traded industrial conglomerate. The parties were married on June 14, 2013, and currently reside at 1847 Birchwood Lane, Winnetka, Illinois 60093.

**Valuation Date.** The effective date of this valuation is November 30, 2024. All analyses, conclusions, and opinions expressed herein are as of that date.

**Standard of Value.** The standard of value applied in this engagement is fair market value, defined as the price at which property would change hands between a willing buyer and a willing seller, neither being under compulsion to buy or sell, and both having reasonable knowledge of relevant facts. This definition is consistent with the standard articulated by the Internal Revenue Service in Revenue Ruling 59-60 and is widely accepted in both legal and valuation professional contexts.

**Premise of Value.** The valuation was performed under the premise of value as a going concern --- that is, the Practice is assumed to continue operating in its current form for the foreseeable future.

**Scope of Review.** In the course of this engagement, Strand Valuation Services reviewed and considered the following sources of information, among others:

> • Audited and internally prepared financial statements of Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC for fiscal years 2019 through 2023, and unaudited financial data through October 2024;
>
> • Federal income tax returns and associated Schedule K-1s for the Practice for fiscal years 2019 through 2023;
>
> • The Amended and Restated Operating Agreement of Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC, dated March 15, 2018;
>
> • Provider compensation surveys and benchmarking data from the Medical Group Management Association (MGMA) and the American Medical Group Association (AMGA);
>
> • Payer mix reports and managed care contract summaries for the Practice;
>
> • Industry publications and market data relating to pediatric cardiology and physician-owned specialty practices;
>
> • Interviews with Dr. Veselka-Thornton and the Practice\'s managing partner regarding operations, growth prospects, competitive dynamics, and strategic planning; and
>
> • Financial disclosures prepared in coordination with Linden & Fosse CPA, the certified public accounting firm retained to prepare financial disclosures for both parties in connection with the postnuptial agreement process.

We note that certain financial data and disclosures were coordinated through Linden & Fosse CPA, and we have relied upon the accuracy and completeness of the information provided by that firm and by the Practice\'s management. Strand Valuation Services has not independently audited or verified the financial information supplied to us.

**[SECTION 3: DESCRIPTION OF THE ENTITY]{.underline}**

**[Overview]{.underline}**

Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC is an Illinois limited liability company organized as a physician-owned medical practice. The Practice provides a comprehensive range of pediatric cardiology and general pediatric services to patients throughout suburban Chicago, Illinois, and the greater Chicagoland metropolitan area. The Practice was founded in approximately 2012 and has been in continuous operation for approximately twelve years as of the valuation date.

**[Services and Clinical Operations]{.underline}**

The Practice\'s core clinical services include pediatric cardiology consultations, diagnostic evaluation and management of congenital and acquired heart disease in children, and ongoing cardiac care coordination. In addition to its cardiology subspecialty, the Practice provides general pediatric services, including well-child examinations, acute illness management, and developmental assessments. A significant component of the Practice\'s revenue is derived from ancillary diagnostic services, including echocardiography (transthoracic and fetal), exercise stress testing, Holter and event monitoring, and electrocardiography. These diagnostic services are performed in-house at both of the Practice\'s clinical locations, providing convenience for patients and an important revenue stream for the Practice.

**[Hospital Affiliation]{.underline}**

The Practice operates in affiliation with Lakeshore Children\'s Hospital, a prominent pediatric institution in suburban Chicago. Several of the Practice\'s physicians, including Dr. Veselka-Thornton, hold active clinical privileges at Lakeshore Children\'s Hospital and provide inpatient consultative services, participate in hospital-based cardiac programs, and contribute to multidisciplinary pediatric care teams. This hospital affiliation is a material component of the Practice\'s referral network and revenue generation.

**[Ownership Structure]{.underline}**

Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC is structured as a multi-physician limited liability company with several physician-members. Dr. Diane Veselka-Thornton holds a 22% membership interest in the LLC. The remaining membership interests are held by the other physician-partners. The Practice\'s current physician complement includes approximately eight to ten physicians --- a combination of pediatric cardiologists and general pediatricians --- supported by nurse practitioners, medical assistants, registered nurses, administrative staff, and billing personnel.

**[Locations]{.underline}**

The Practice operates from two clinical locations in suburban Cook County, Illinois. Both locations are leased premises, and the leases are held in the name of the Practice.

**[Operating Agreement Provisions Relevant to Valuation]{.underline}**

The Amended and Restated Operating Agreement of Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC, dated March 15, 2018, contains several provisions material to the valuation of a minority membership interest. These include:

> • **Restrictions on Transfer.** Membership interests may not be transferred, assigned, or encumbered without the prior written consent of members holding at least two-thirds of the outstanding membership interests. Any proposed transfer is subject to a right of first refusal by the remaining members, exercisable within ninety days of written notice.
>
> • **Non-Compete Covenants.** Departing members are subject to a non-competition covenant restricting the practice of pediatric cardiology within a defined geographic radius for a period of two years following departure.
>
> • **Mandatory Buy-Sell Provisions.** The Operating Agreement contains mandatory buy-sell provisions triggered upon the death, permanent disability, or voluntary withdrawal of a member. The buy-sell price is determined by a formula based on a trailing three-year average of the Practice\'s net revenues, subject to adjustment by mutual agreement or independent appraisal.

These transfer restrictions and buy-sell provisions are relevant factors in the determination of appropriate valuation discounts, as discussed in Section 7 of this report.

**[SECTION 4: ECONOMIC AND INDUSTRY OVERVIEW]{.underline}**

**[General Economic Conditions]{.underline}**

As of November 2024, the United States economy continues to experience moderate growth, with real GDP expanding at an annualized rate of approximately 2.5% in the third quarter of 2024. The Federal Reserve has maintained a cautious posture with respect to monetary policy, and inflation has moderated toward the central bank\'s target. The suburban Chicago metropolitan area has experienced economic conditions broadly consistent with national trends, with low unemployment, stable consumer spending, and continued growth in the healthcare sector.

**[Healthcare Industry Landscape]{.underline}**

The healthcare services industry in the greater Chicagoland area remains robust, characterized by steady demand for physician services, ongoing consolidation among hospital systems and physician groups, and gradual shifts in reimbursement models from fee-for-service toward value-based care arrangements. Physician-owned specialty practices in the suburbs of Chicago continue to operate in a favorable demand environment, supported by population growth, an aging demographic profile among certain patient populations, and expanded insurance coverage under the Affordable Care Act and state Medicaid programs.

**[Pediatric Cardiology Market Conditions]{.underline}**

The pediatric cardiology subspecialty occupies a favorable position within the broader healthcare services market. Demand for pediatric cardiology services in the suburban Chicago market has grown steadily over the review period, driven by several factors:

**Insurance Reimbursement Rate Increases.** Over the five-year period from 2019 through 2024, commercial insurance reimbursement rates for pediatric cardiology services --- including evaluation and management codes, echocardiography, and other diagnostic procedures --- have increased by approximately 12% to 18% nationally. These rate increases have been driven by annual adjustments to the Medicare Physician Fee Schedule published by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS), commercial insurer benchmarking to CMS rates, and renegotiation of managed care contracts at the regional level. These are industry-wide phenomena reflecting systemic changes in payer reimbursement, and they are not attributable to the marketing, negotiation, or clinical efforts of any individual physician practice.

**Demographic Market Expansion.** The suburban communities of Cook County, Illinois, and adjacent collar counties have experienced meaningful population growth in the family-age demographic --- households with children aged 0 through 18 --- over the review period. According to data from the U.S. Census Bureau and the Illinois Department of Public Health, the pediatric population in the Practice\'s primary service area grew by approximately 6% to 8% between 2019 and 2024. This demographic expansion has resulted in increased patient volumes for pediatric specialty practices, independent of any individual practice\'s physician recruitment or marketing initiatives.

**Ancillary Diagnostic Service Demand.** Utilization of echocardiographic studies and other cardiac diagnostic procedures in the pediatric population has increased in response to evolving clinical practice guidelines issued by the American Academy of Pediatrics and the American College of Cardiology, expanded screening recommendations for student-athletes, and greater awareness among primary care providers of the need for early cardiac evaluation. These trends have contributed to increased diagnostic volumes across the pediatric cardiology sector.

**[Regulatory Environment]{.underline}**

No material adverse regulatory changes affecting the Practice\'s operations or reimbursement were identified during the review period. The Practice maintains compliance with all applicable state licensure, credentialing, and billing regulations.

**[Competitive Environment]{.underline}**

Direct competition in pediatric cardiology within the Practice\'s service area is limited. The number of board-certified pediatric cardiologists in suburban Cook County is relatively small, and barriers to entry are significant due to the subspecialty credentialing requirements, the need for hospital affiliations, and the capital investment required for in-house diagnostic equipment. These competitive dynamics support the Practice\'s continued market position and are favorable factors in the valuation.

**[Summary of Financial Performance]{.underline}**

The following table presents a summary of the Practice\'s financial performance for the five-year period from 2019 through 2024. The 2024 figures are annualized based on actual results through October 31, 2024.

  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Fiscal Year**   **Total Revenue**   **Operating Expenses**   **EBITDA**     **Operating Margin**
  ----------------- ------------------- ------------------------ -------------- ----------------------
  2019              \$8,200,000         \$5,820,000              \$2,380,000    29.0%
